findings.title: flattenSpecialties splits legitimate comma-containing specialty names
findings.why_it_matters: flattenSpecialties splits on comma/semicolon/pipe. A specialty like 'Systems Design, Architecture & Planning' would be incorrectly split into two entries: 'Systems Design' and 'Architecture & Planning'. Users who import author data with comma-containing specialties will see them fragmented. No escape mechanism exists for literal commas.
evidence: Regex /[,;|]/ at src/lib/authors.ts:41 splits on comma
Test confirms split behavior: ['A, B, C'] → ['A', 'B', 'C']
No quoting or escaping mechanism in the function

residual_risks: flattenSpecialties has O(n*m) where n=input array size, m=max tokens per entry. For adversarial input with 10,000 long specialty entries, this could cause frame drops during render. Mitigated by typical author data having <20 specialties each.
